headline: Attackers strip-mine security vendors' own appliances for credentials as OpenAI gates its newest model behind government cyber safeguards.

emerging_trends.text: Frontier AI labs are walling off their most capable systems behind government engagement and cyber safeguards, with OpenAI's GPT-5.6 reaching only a handful of vetted companies.
emerging_trends.topic_title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 With Cyber Safeguards

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep mining security vendors' own gear for login secrets, and the FortiGate harvesting that reached hundreds of thousands of firewalls now draws fresh defensive playbooks.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Large-Scale Credential Attacks Target Security Appliances

emerging_trends.text: Surveillance technology is creeping into law enforcement and the military, as Meta prototypes real-time facial recognition with a Pentagon supplier.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Meta Tests Facial Recognition for Police and Military

topics.title: Large-Scale Credential Attacks Target Security Appliances
topics.latest_developments: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 published a threat brief on June 26 laying out how organizations blunt large-scale credential attacks, the guidance aimed squarely at campaigns like FortiBleed that drained 110 million logins from 430,000 FortiGate firewalls.
topics.summary: Credential-harvesting operations increasingly hit the perimeter devices security vendors sell, FortiGate firewalls above all, turning stolen logins into footholds; defenders should rotate exposed credentials, enforce multi-factor authentication, and hunt for rogue sniffers.

sources.title: Threat Brief: Mitigating Large-Scale Credential Attacks
Week in review: Fortibleed campaign’s impact on orgs, Cisco Unified CM flaw exploited

topics.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 With Cyber Safeguards
topics.latest_developments: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 in three versions—Sol, Terra, and Luna—as a limited preview to a small number of companies, restricting access and adding stronger cyber safeguards as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government.
topics.summary: Sol stands as the flagship and most powerful, Terra balances efficiency and power, and Luna tunes for speed and affordability; OpenAI's curbed rollout reflects worry that frontier models sharpen offensive cyber capability.

sources.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards

topics.title: AI Reshapes the Cybersecurity Workforce
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading reported that confidence in autonomous AI penetration testing is slipping as fewer companies lean on the technology to find weaknesses, while a separate analysis argued AI opens new entry-level cybersecurity roles for candidates with strong human judgment.
topics.summary: Security teams are recalibrating where AI helps: automated pen testing draws fresh skepticism even as human decision-making anchors early-career work.

sources.title: AI Decline? Confidence in Autonomous Penetration Testing Falls
AI Won't Wipe-Out Entry-Level Cybersecurity Jobs

topics.title: Meta Tests Facial Recognition for Police and Military
topics.latest_developments: Bruce Schneier flagged that Meta is prototyping real-time facial recognition for police and military use with a Pentagon supplier, even as Immigration and Customs Enforcement seeks eyeglasses that identify people on sight.
topics.summary: The effort would put live identity matching into the hands of officers and soldiers, raising surveillance and civil-liberties stakes around wearable recognition gear.

sources.title: Meta Is Testing Facial Recognition for Police and Military

topics.title: Russia Accuses Apple of Censorship Over VK Removal
topics.latest_developments: Apple pulled VK's flagship VKontakte along with VK Music, VK Messenger, VK Video, Odnoklassniki, and Mail.ru's email app from the App Store, and Russia accused the company of political censorship.
topics.summary: The removals strip Russian iOS users of the country's dominant social and email apps; Moscow frames Apple's move as politically motivated.

sources.title: Russia accuses Apple of ‘political censorship’ after VK apps removed from App Store

topics.title: Open Source Sustainability Initiative Tackles End-of-Life Software
topics.latest_developments: The Open Source Sustainability Initiative launched to help enterprises manage and secure aging, end-of-life open source projects while holding to regulatory compliance.
topics.summary: End-of-life open source components keep shipping inside enterprise software without maintainers or patches; the initiative aims to track and secure them before attackers do.

sources.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software

weather: Today: Patchy Fog then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 85F.
Tonight: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms then Partly Cloudy, low 67F.

games.recap: Eugenio Suárez hits 3-run homer in 9th as Reds rally for 9-7 win over Pirates

around_town.title: Lancaster Measles Outbreak Defies Containment
around_town.latest_developments: A June 28 Post-Gazette report found that lingering distrust and low vaccination rates threaten efforts to contain the measles outbreak centered in Lancaster County.
around_town.summary: Public-health workers in Lancaster County are struggling to halt a measles outbreak as vaccine skepticism and low immunization rates let the disease spread, part of a widening Pennsylvania case count that has already reached Northumberland County.

sources.title: Lingering distrust, low vaccine rates threaten containment of Lancaster measles outbreak

around_town.title: Thousands Pack Point State Park for America 250 Concert
around_town.latest_developments: Thousands filled Point State Park on June 27 for the America250PA Commonwealth Concert headlined by Nelly and Third Eye Blind, which organizers called the largest free statewide concert series staged for the nation's 250th anniversary.
around_town.summary: Pittsburgh and America250PA drew thousands through rain to Point State Park downtown for a free Commonwealth Concert headlined by Nelly and Third Eye Blind, marking the United States Semiquincentennial.

sources.title: Thousands pack Point State Park for historic America 250 concert
Thousands celebrate America's 250 birthday at free Point State Park concert

around_town.title: ACT UP Presses for HIV Funding in Pittsburgh
around_town.latest_developments: A June 28 Post-Gazette report detailed a local ACT UP group's awareness campaign warning that 'AIDS is not over' as federal HIV funding faces cuts.
around_town.summary: A Pittsburgh ACT UP chapter mounted a public campaign arguing AIDS remains a live threat and pressing to protect HIV funding against proposed federal reductions.

sources.title: 'AIDS is not over:' Behind a local group's awareness campaign for HIV funding

around_teams.title: Pirates Activate Konnor Griffin
around_teams.latest_developments: The Pittsburgh Pirates activated top prospect Konnor Griffin from the injured list June 26, ending the rehab assignment he had begun at Double-A Altoona.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette reported the Pirates brought infield prospect Konnor Griffin off the injured list to reinforce a roster thinned by injuries, and he homered leading off in his return.

sources.title: Pirates activate Konnor Griffin from the IL, providing much-needed reinforcements

around_teams.title: Don Kelly Reaches 100th Win as Pirates Manager
around_teams.latest_developments: A June 26 Post-Gazette feature marked Pittsburgh Pirates manager Don Kelly's 100th career managerial victory.
around_teams.summary: The Pirates celebrated manager Don Kelly's 100th win, with players saying 'he deserves it' and hoping the milestone points toward a postseason push.

sources.title: 'He deserves it': The Pirates celebrate Don Kelly's 100th win — and hope more are on the way

around_teams.title: Pirates Open Legacy Hall at PNC Park
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette previewed June 25 the Pirates' new Legacy Hall at PNC Park, a fan space assembling the franchise's history.
around_teams.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates' new Legacy Hall at PNC Park will let fans young and old 'see a lot of history,' gathering the team's past into an interactive exhibit at the ballpark.

sources.title: The Pirates' new Legacy Hall at PNC Park will allow fans young and old to 'see a lot of history'

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: Notes From The Bubble, Volume 1
reading.summary: Zitron launches an ongoing series of dispatches cataloguing what he reads as warning signs that the AI investment bubble is beginning to unravel.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the company and why Field sees AI as a tailwind rather than a threat to design software.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport argues AI companies should stop hyping their own products as dangerous as a marketing tactic, likening it to Ford publishing a whitepaper warning its F-150 is alarmingly unsafe.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
